journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan - replace old note with new note
journal_entry.rationale: This is a loan refinancing transaction where the old loan obligation of $100,000 is extinguished and replaced with a new loan obligation of $100,000. We debit (decrease) the old Loan Payable liability by $100,000 and credit (increase) a new Loan Payable liability by $100,000. This represents a liability-for-liability exchange with no net change to total liabilities or cash position. No tax applies to debt refinancing transactions.

output.reason: Refinancing replaces an old loan with a new loan. The debit side records the extinguishment of the old $100 loan liability, which is a liability decrease. (The credit side would record the new $100 loan liability increase, but we only classify debits here.)

debit_classifier.reason: Refinancing replaces an old loan with a new loan. The debit side records the extinguishment of the old $100 loan liability, which is a liability decrease. (The credit side would record the new $100 loan liability increase, but we only classify debits here.)

output.reason: Refinancing replaces one liability with another. On the credit side: the old loan is removed (liability decrease), which would normally be a debit, but we only classify credits. The new loan of $100 is established (liability increase). Credit side shows: 1 line for new loan liability increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Refinancing replaces one liability with another. On the credit side: the old loan is removed (liability decrease), which would normally be a debit, but we only classify credits. The new loan of $100 is established (liability increase). Credit side shows: 1 line for new loan liability increase.
